text: A 2-week motivational self-talk intervention significantly improved cycling time-to-exhaustion at 80% peak power output by approximately 18% (from 637 to 750 seconds) in recreational athletes.
Motivational self-talk significantly reduces rating of perceived exertion (RPE) during endurance exercise, with RPE dropping from 7.3 to 6.4 at 50% isotime during high-intensity cycling.
specific_value: RPE reduced from 7.3 to 6.4 at 50% isotime

text: Perception of effort is a key determinant of endurance performance, and psychological interventions that reduce perceived effort can extend time to exhaustion without changes in physiological capacity.
Motivational self-talk can be learned and applied effectively within a 2-week training period to produce measurable endurance performance benefits.
Without a psychological intervention, endurance time-to-exhaustion performance does not improve from repeated testing alone, as the control group showed no change (486 vs 474 seconds).
